title: build,config: add support for `--unsetannotation`
body:
Just like `--unsetlabel` add support for `--unsetannotation`.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6183

```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *flouthoc* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
How does this affect caching and cache evaluation?

@nalind `--unsetannotation` must invalidate the cache if used and the build should remove annotation from final image instead of using any previous image from the cache, I have added a test to verify this.

> The history now only notes that the annotation was suppressed at the final instruction of a stage, while it will have been suppressed at the first instruction in a stage.

Technically annotation only exists at `base` image there is no other way to add annotation apart from CLI flag, from that perspective. Does it make more sense to add it to history of `first step` of a stage which contains annotation ? and not the `last step` and `last instruction` ? But one problem which it can cause is making all the lower steps and lower stages not valid cache hits even though only annotation part did not match. I wonder if that is a good idea ?

The tests should include a case where there are no instructions in the Dockerfile after the FROM line, e.g.:
```Dockerfile
FROM busybox
```
I think there's some logic needed for that case that we don't have yet.

> The tests should include a case where there are no instructions in the Dockerfile after the FROM line, e.g.:
> 
> ```dockerfile
> FROM busybox
> ```
> 
> I think there's some logic needed for that case that we don't have yet.

Done added case for this, also added test. However `--layers` will not work with this because there are no layers so we cannot write history. We can write `empty_layer` and store history there but no sure if it is needed for this patch and use-case.

> Done added case for this, also added test. However `--layers` will not work with this because there are no layers so we cannot write history. We can write `empty_layer` and store history there but no sure if it is needed for this patch and use-case.

Why shouldn't it produce _some_ image, when `--layers` is the default in `podman build`?

> > Done added case for this, also added test. However `--layers` will not work with this because there are no layers so we cannot write history. We can write `empty_layer` and store history there but no sure if it is needed for this patch and use-case.
> 
> Why shouldn't it produce _some_ image, when `--layers` is the default in `podman build`?

Oh maybe I phrased it wronly, it produces an image but it does not results in returning the same image again when built again.

For example

```
FROM busybox
```

```
buildah --unsetannotation org.opencontainers.image.name build --layers -t test .
```

Produces an image but if I do this again

```
buildah --unsetannotation org.opencontainers.image.name build --layers -t test .
```

it produces a new sha instead of returning the old one, since nothing has changed. Since our cache checking logic clicks in only for builds with more than `1` steps in it.

> That new test case raises the question of whether or not it's a bug that we don't set an identity label when a build doesn't have instructions that force us to commit a new image, but this at least LGTM.

Okay I will open a different issue for this since it is not related to this PR. @containers/buildah-maintainers PTAL
